claims.evidence_text: the threshold level of salinity tolerance of wheat is 4–6 dSm/m... saline irrigation water (with EC- 4dSm/m) was mixed with good quality water.
claims.claim_summary: Salinity tolerance threshold of wheat is an EC of 4-6 dS/m (unit reported as dS/m, not percent).

claims.evidence_text: Wheat is only moderately tolerant to salinity; the loss in its grain yield exceeds 60% under saline conditions.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat is only moderately salt-tolerant; grain yield loss can exceed 60% under saline conditions.

claims.evidence_text: the recommended N, P2O5, and K2O were applied at the rate of 150, 60, and 40 kg ha−1 in the form of Urea, Single Super Phosphate (SSP), and Muriate of Potash (MOP), respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Recommended nitrogen rate for wheat (India saline trial) was 150 kg N/ha, applied in split doses.

claims.evidence_text: the recommended N, P2O5, and K2O were applied at the rate of 150, 60, and 40 kg ha−1
claims.claim_summary: Recommended phosphorus (P2O5) rate for wheat (India saline trial) was 60 kg/ha.

claims.evidence_text: the recommended N, P2O5, and K2O were applied at the rate of 150, 60, and 40 kg ha−1
claims.claim_summary: Recommended potassium (K2O) rate for wheat (India saline trial) was 40 kg/ha.

claims.evidence_text: LAI... was the highest during 69–90 DAS. At 69 and 90 DAS, the highest LAI was observed in genotypes ASN_SAN_17 (3.62) and ASN_SAN_06 (3.65).
claims.claim_summary: Peak wheat leaf area index under saline field conditions was 3.62-3.65 (dimensionless) at 69-90 days after sowing.
